question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: internalPaths] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to `/metrics` (an `internalPaths` entry logged at `log.Debug`) to silently read Prometheus metrics that expose the current number of in-flight requests (`hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight`), using this data to time a burst attack that maximizes gossip suppression when the server is near capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` body that is never terminated, causing the inner handler to block indefinitely while `TracingMiddleware` holds the goroutine, creating a resource exhaustion condition that prevents transaction gossip submissions from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to a string containing format verbs (e.g., `%s`, `%d`, `%v`), which are then passed to `fmt.Sprintf` in `TracingMiddleware`, and if the format string is not properly escaped, trigger a format string vulnerability that causes unexpected behavior or information disclosure related to transaction gossip state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path containing `%00` (null byte) or other URL-encoded special characters that are decoded by `request.URL.RequestURI()` and embedded in the log message, causing log parsing failures in downstream systems that monitor transaction gossip activity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `TracingMiddleware` does not validate the HTTP method, sending requests with custom or oversized method strings that are logged via `request.Method`, causing log entries to be malformed and breaking automated log analysis tools that detect gossip suppression patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: tracingResponseWriter.Write()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response path where the inner handler calls `Write()` with a nil or zero-length byte slice, causing `w.data` to be set to an empty slice, and the logged status code to remain 200, creating a false impression that transaction gossip submissions are succeeding when they are actually failing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the inner handler to call `Write()` before `WriteHeader()`, relying on Go's implicit 200 status, so that `tracingResponseWriter.statusCode` is never explicitly set and always shows 200 in logs, hiding transaction gossip errors from operators?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getClientIpAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user set `X-Real-IP` to an IPv6 address with a scope zone ID (e.g., `fe80::1%eth0`), which is not validated by `getClientIpAddress()` and is logged verbatim, causing IP-based rate limiting or blocking systems to fail to match the address and allowing the attacker to bypass per-IP limits on gossip submission requests?
